reviews.review_text:
Fun.

Would be awesome if it was possible to co-op online, where each player has their own screen and both players can use mouse and keyboard.

Probably the steepest learning curve I've ever seen in a video game. It's just downright obscene how unforgiving this game can be for new players. Not just the bullet hellish combat that has you dodging 100s of projectiles on the screen, but also how cryptic the game can be at times. Items have vague descriptions that may not make it clear as to what they do, then you use it and summon a bunch of enemies. The game punishes you for saving items for later by having this stupid rat steal your ammo that you may not need at the moment but could use later. It's a brutal and kinda evil experience that keeps you playing with its quirky and goofy style. 

And then you start to get good, weave through attacks without rolling, no-hit bosses which grants you better loot and a heart container, learn how to use each gun and discover synergies between guns and items, triage your guns for room clearing and for bosses, and finally you get that first clear. Only to realize that you've basically scratched the surface of what this game has to offer. Welcome to the most extensive endgame ever, it nearly never ends with its secret areas, multiple secret bosses, like 4 secret characters, and new game modes. Now it's got you hooked. 

While the overall game is a blast, there are a few annoyances, hopefully they get addressed in the sequel. The game is heavily dependent on skill over luck, you can beat the game with your unlimited-yet-weak starting gun, but the fun you'll have is luck dependent. I've had runs where I'd keep getting ammo drops on the first floor where they're useless, and never get ammo again. Or where the game would just decided I don't deserve anything except the lowest tier chests. In other rougelikes like Hades or Dead Cells, I felt like I was able to strategically craft great builds with any combination, but here there are runs where I genuinely felt screwed over the entire way through with just bad luck. And while I could just use my starting weapon, it's just not as fun as using all the other super creative guns. 

It's a minor gripe but keeps me from playing this game as much as my other favorite action roguelikes. Still a classic that you must play if you like action rougelikes.

I so badly want to enjoy this game, but the camera makes me basically immediately dizzy and I can't find a fix in the settings nor has the solution I've found online worked.
I cannot personally recommend the game with this in mind, but I do absolutely see the appeal from what I've been able to play.

For the first time in years, I'll be leaving a game unfinished.

After 13 or so hours, I can safely say the rogue-like genre isn't for me. I enjoyed a few runs here and there, but the lack of permanent upgrades, as seen in a rogue-lite, and the emphasis on horizontal progression, reduced my enjoyment on each subsequent run.

This isn't to say the game is awful, I actually think it's great. The sheer amount of content alone is impressive. Each character felt unique, and I know there's a bunch more you can unlock, such as the Robot.

It's a shame I had to drop the game this way, but I'm not stubborn enough to put another 30, maybe 40, hours into something I'm not having the best time with. I'll stick to rogue-lites and souls-likes from now on!

Enter the Gungeon is absolute chaos, and that’s exactly why it’s so good.
At first it looks like just another roguelike shooter, but once you start unlocking weapons, discovering synergies, and surviving deeper runs, it becomes incredibly addictive. The game constantly throws ridiculous guns, insane bullet patterns, and nonstop action at you while somehow still staying fair most of the time.

The biggest strength of the game is its weapon variety. Nearly every gun feels unique, and half the fun comes from discovering what ridiculous nonsense you’ll find next. One run you’re using a shotgun shell that shoots shotguns, and the next you’re fighting bosses with a literal mailbox or a gun that fires bees.

Despite all the chaos, the gameplay is surprisingly skill-based. Dodge rolling through massive bullet hell patterns while managing resources and positioning feels incredibly satisfying once you improve.

The good:
- Huge variety of unique weapons
- Extremely addictive gameplay loop
- Tight and responsive controls
- Bullet hell combat feels satisfying
- Tons of unlockables and secrets
- Great humor and weapon designs
- Runs feel different almost every time
- Excellent replayability
- Boss fights are fantastic
- Co-op mode is fun

The bad:
- Very difficult for new players
- RNG can sometimes ruin runs
- Progression can feel slow early on
- Some weapons are far stronger than others
- Bullet clutter can become overwhelming
- Later floors get brutally difficult
- Certain enemies feel annoying rather than challenging
- Can become frustrating if you dislike roguelikes

Overall, Enter the Gungeon is one of the best roguelikes out there. It’s difficult, chaotic, funny, and endlessly replayable. Once the gameplay clicks, it becomes incredibly hard to stop doing “just one more run.”

The game is good but the drop rates are INSANELY bad, hope they improve it and also add more playable characters and some nice permanent buffs that make the game more variable! Or at least improve this in the sequel
This game is like if you want to get cucked in real time but its a fair cuck and pretty cool one at that
Very fun game with a lot of great, genuinely creative artwork, and just general design. The music is also fantastic, but most importantly, the gameplay is very exciting.
One of my top roguelikes. A lot of content that is almost all very fun.
One of the best bullet hell roguelikes on the market. At first you suck, then without warning you become a gun god.
♥♥♥♥ and ball torture in a game format

If you love spending 30+ minutes on one run,
getting to the final level,
having an amazing loadout,
just to die to some stupid floor hazard on the ground cause you weren't paying attention,
this game is for you! 10/10

A great game and worth picking up if you haven't done so yet. The game is harsh as many bullet hell games tend to be but is rewarding once you get the hang of it. It might be a bit overtuned if you are just looking for a chill game but its large loadout of weapons and synergies keep the runs fresh as you spend time finding out what works for you.

Still my favorite roguelike, the skill ceiling's high but the rewards are too. Probably one of the best games to dig your teeth into as you're watching stuff on the side or genuinely looking for a great Danmaku / Bullet Hell game. Only detraction I can think of to some, is that It still has a lot of things I couldnt get to in my nearly 5 years of playing this game, due to how difficult the unlock stipulations are LMFAO.

I beat the game (or rather I got to credits). I can feel the heavy binding of isaac inspiration because I am a skilled player in that game. The learning curve is brutal I don't think this should be anyone's first 2D bullethell roguelike. The game honestly demands way too much skill from the player, but in the end it's probably gonna be a gamebreaking synergy that will get you your first win.
Fun to beat the "base" game but I guess the runs don't feel different enough for me to bother finding more secrets or whatever.

100% deserves to be in the HOF of best Roguelikes, the game deserves every bit of praise. Moddability, charm, and so, so, SO many guns. 100% one of the best roguelikes I've played, and I'm glad there's a bunch of content and gamemodes to enjoy it further!
Peak, Never gets old. So many rare anomalies that I still sometimes find something new during a run. Gameplay definitely 10/10. It can run on any system too.

Holds up (heh) well.

One of the only roguelikes that I actually like (in case you're curious, Wizard of Legend, legacy, 2018 is the other). As of this review, I completed the gungeon to the final floor, done most of the challenges. Twice. Before this, in 2018/9 I played it 100% on PS4, though the steamdeck is a lot more addictive. You think: "Just one more run," which becomes really, how about I devote the next afternoon off.

This game isn't easy, though I noticed in other reviews the difficulty lies in the curve and understanding the mechanics. Bullets are telegraphed well, dodging can save most direct hits, there are a ton of synergies in the weapons/upgrades - it values experimentation and holding onto things just in case. So while I would caution the difficulty somewhat, especially if you have motor/coordination control difficulty, this is one of the best dungeon games ever. 

And the puns - a chicken flute is called "Foul Play." Need I say more? This game oozes charm. I suspect it might go on sale occasionally, but still worth full price imo. 

My playtime is minimised because I played it in airplane mode on the steamdeck, so my initial play-through on PS4 was over 100 Hrs, this was more like 30 hrs almost 100%ing again, and a standard play-through/run is around 20-50 minutes. Mileage varies greatly, so keep that in mind.

After 20+ hours for 5 years playing,I finally beat the draguns.I only happen to spend that much time because I always give up out of frustration along that 5 years.Until this month I started playing this game,its getting addicting collecting those guns,unique item and most importantly,attempting to WIN in this game.I started to think that this game is my coping mechanism to escape the reality of my ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ job,that reminds me of a failure I am deciding to work at the place I hate instead of achieving something in my life.Thats where the source of addiction in this game.I need to WIN.I want to be BETTER at something.Thats it back to the new run of Enter The Gungeon !
amazing game...pretty hard to 100% easy to beat the 1st few times...its get alot more fun after beating the 1st past

I can't ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ take it anymore. I have been trying to unlock the Gunslinger for so long i don't remember my own name. I got 3 attempts at unlocking him, each run was worse than the last, one lost to RNG the other ones because of skill issue since i had to do a 1 hour run before that of course. 10/10 game, love it to death.
great game and very challenging! took me plenty of hours just to unlock the last boss! amazeeee!
its js heat bro, trust me, bullet hell of any year, even since its release, mods also make it more plausable.

It's weird to owe a debt of gratitude to a video game.

Nearly every hour of my playtime was on the clock, during "remote" work pandemic days much in need of distraction. My obsession with this game at that time had me browsing the category on Twitch, which connected me to my favorite streamer by complete fluke, someone I still watch to this day. 

Anytime I boot this game back up I remember that something can just be perfectly, thoroughly, fun. Very few games leave the player to stand on their own like this one.

amazing game, good enough to play for hundreds of hours and unlock everything (except for that ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ high dragunfire) and complete all achievements
well worth the price
difficult in all the best ways and forgiving enough to learn as a first bullet hell
It's always fun to replay this game over and over to train your habit of focusing in gaming, Failure would be always the greatest teacher

Very fun! Its a little difficult when you start, but once you get the hang of things it becomes awesome.

This is a very good game, but the core progression is a bit flawed. By giving all of the materials to unlock an elevator which allows you to start runs on later floors, you will lock yourself out on any weapons, upgrade, and cash that can be given on the previous levels. I personally always start my runs on the first floor, but it get's so tiring playing through the same place over and over again so that I can have a head start for the future floors. But that's honestly my biggest gripe with the game, other then that, I love the gameplay, the fun weapons, cool bosses and enemies, and the great art direction. I really hope enter the gungeon 2 can fix this issue to avoid being repetitive. I wish to keep playing this game, but that one flaw makes it really hard for me to jump into the game.

I found out that I like neither the controls of top-down shooters or the gameplay loop of roguelikes after playing this game, but that isn't the games fault, it's mine. Plus it looks pretty. There's also a good chance I'll play it in like a year and sink 100 hours into it by accident but only time will tell.

A really addictive and fun game, that has excellent soundtrack, solid mechanics, and challenging fights. For me it took 175 hours to unlock all items, and I can confidently say it was very much worth it. If you are looking for a solid roguelike with bullet hell, buy this one. It will very likely be worth your money.

Good game. Wish there was more variety in setting, a lot of it takes place in this mineshaft-esque aesthetic. Could also benefit more from additional game modes. However, still pretty fun overall. I only got to stage 3 once after playing for 4 hours.

Insanely difficult and often insanely frustrating, but still 10x more addictive. A densely-packed game full of content and endless replayability. A humble little masterpiece, especially when taking the price and absolutely tiny install size into account.
This game is fun, but at some point it became really repetitive. Even though it's roguelike, there's not much synergies or something like that. Mostly, difference between runs are different guns.

8/10
Really fun roguelite albeit a bit difficult at first with little progression in the form of new unlocked items that carry over to future runs. Other than that its pure skill and knowledge that you gain through multiple runs. Its really satfisying once you start to make progress and eventually complete a run. Also the pixel art is looks amazing.

A true classic. It may run on spaghetti code but it's my spaghetti code. That is to say mods aren't super stable but the unmodded experience is great. This pushed the popularization of roguelikes a LOT and has a banger story to add to the fun gameplay. The systems may not be super balanced but they make me happy
sensationally addictive; making progress in this game is like crack

Unique and timeless. I don't think I can describe the feeling you get from this game, going through and unlocking everything for the first time. I just had no idea what to expect. From the graphics to the music and sound design, to the level design and ambience, Enter the Gungeon is something else. You'd play a few floors, get killed by a mimic that comes out of the wall, and you don't think "what the hell?" you think "ope, should've known better." The game has a great skill ceiling to the point where many of my new runs I try not to take any damage at all. I don't win, obviously. Even after 10! floors of perfect variety, bullet hell chaos, and careful strategy, I'm left wanting more. And the only way to get more, is to go back and do it again. 10/10, and you better not mess up the second game fellas.

I forgot to get the bullet to kill the past in this run that I had top level RNG in and steamrolled dragun immediately, wanted to test the physics on the shell waterfall and got sucked in by the door hitbox and now im sad cuz i wanted to kill the green pig
frustrating game that doesn't even have half decent multiplayer (player 2 is locked to a specific boring character and mp is local only)

Enter the Gungeon is a competent and well made roguelite that I was done with before the first run finished and I'm not entirely sure that's the game's fault.

Dodge Roll built something with genuine craft and a clear love for the genre. The gun pun lore is committed to in a way that rewards curiosity, the pixel art is sharp and the color palette has a personality that most games in this space don't bother developing, and the bullet hell mechanics are tight enough that the ceiling for skilled players is genuinely high. None of that is in question.

The problem is the genre has been so thoroughly colonized since 2016 that arriving late to Enter the Gungeon means you've already absorbed most of what it pioneered through everything that came after it. The dodge roll mechanic, the item synergy hunting, the escalating difficulty, the meta progression unlocks, all of it feels familiar in a way that isn't the game's fault but does blunt its impact considerably depending on what you've played before touching it.

Coming in already fatigued by the genre means the steep early difficulty reads as friction rather than invitation, and for a game that asks real time investment before it opens up that first impression carries a lot of weight.

Dodge Roll followed this up with Exit the Gungeon and have been quiet since, which given how thoroughly their debut influenced the landscape around it is its own kind of legacy.

On sale on Steam Deck is the right context if you're going to give it a shot. Short sessions, low stakes, see if it finds you.

I respect the variety of guns and equipment. The bosses are cool and so is the Dodge Roll, and I do like how most of the guns feel to use. But I really feel like the runs are, more often than not, just a waste of time. If you don't find an NPC or something and aren't goated, you're probably just gonna die and come back to the hub with very little(if anything) to show for your efforts. Probably unlocking a gun or two that you either don't even think seem interesting or will barely ever find because there's so many. The different characters are alright but nothing special, nowhere near as interesting or different as those in something like Slay the Spire or Monster Train. The music here is genuinely great though, I'll definitely give that to the composer
it's fine, not amazing, but i enjoy it. gives panic and screaming sometimes, but kinda unforgiving and annoying. it's a Yes anyways. but dont expect anything else.

i have never once in my life left a review on a video game and I'm never going to do it again but this game is genuinely THAT good. 

after buying ETG on 3 separate platforms and 100% completing it on one, i can say that its probably the best game I've ever played. so good that I'm replaying the entire thing over again from scratch. i would pay $300 for this game if that's what they charged but its literally $15. 

its better than all of the sex I've had in my life combined. I'm dead serious. buy the game

To take a snippet from some others reviews:
"Probably the steepest learning curve I've ever seen in a video game. It's just downright obscene how unforgiving this game can be for new players. Not just the bullet hellish combat that has you dodging 100s of projectiles on the screen, but also how cryptic the game can be at times. Items have vague descriptions that may not make it clear as to what they do, then you use it and summon a bunch of enemies. The game punishes you for saving items for later by having this stupid rat steal your ammo that you may not need at the moment but could use later. It's a brutal and kinda evil experience that keeps you playing with its quirky and goofy style."

And:
"The bad:
- Very difficult for new players
- RNG can sometimes ruin runs
- Progression can feel slow early on
- Some weapons are far stronger than others
- Bullet clutter can become overwhelming
- Later floors get brutally difficult
- Certain enemies feel annoying rather than challenging"

These two people left POSITIVE reviews. I'm only 10hrs in but it's giving me no desire to spend however many hours more it takes to get to the good stuff when I'm already not having fun. I absolutely want at least some form of description and information or numbers on basic mechanics or items and there's just... not. 
Props to those who enjoy it. Maybe I'll just watch some videos to get to see some of it and spend my time on other games that I enjoy.

spawn. die in first room. try again. die to first boss. try again. get to 3rd floor rolling! die horribly. try again. die in first room...

will never not enjoy. truly great. 10/10

naming your company after a good game mechanic made into the most useless move in your game is a great metaphor for how the game has all the puzzle pieces for an incredible game and then it's just assembled in all the wrong ways to become a really unfun and unsatisfying experience

I see the effort put into this game. It's very funny to look at all the gun and bullet themed details in the game: Walking bullets shooting with guns, the puns, the item descriptions. The music is really good, I still sometimes hear it when going around doing my business. I like the basic gameplay: Shooting enemies while dodging and blocking the bullet hell around you. Hell, I had fun trying to figure out dodging patterns with minimal movement.

I spent around 13 hours on this game. I got to the boss at the fifth chamber once. Despite not having experienced all the game has to offer, I can safely say I got my money's worth, even if I had paid the full price. Quite cheap for the amount of content!

The game currently has an overall rating of "Overwhelmingly positive". I'm giving a thumbs-down because I don't think Enter the Gungeon is [i]that[/i] good.

Here's my major gripe with the game: For a roguelike, each run of Enter the Gungeon feels very similar. Sure, room layouts change, different enemies spawn, you get different guns... But none of that makes a run feel [i]unique[/i]. Every gun I found was certainly better than the starting guns, but none of them felt anything special. They have different properties, like reloading time, rate of fire, magazine size - all the basic stuff, really - but the effects to the gameplay were very minimal for a roguelike. Sometimes a gun had a special property like piercing or poison, but it barely made any difference. Every encounter boiled down to the same: Shoot enemies and dodge their shots.

This is quite different to other roguelikes I've played, where learning to utilize all the stuff you get is what makes the difference. In Enter the Gungeon, all you can really do is learn to aim and dodge better. This makes Enter the Gungeon feel more like the old, difficult arcade games like Metal Slug where death means you start the entire game over (or you put a coin in the machine to continue).

The 13 hours I spent on this game was spread over three attempts over several years. Each attempt ended with these same thoughts. I can see the game has a lot more content to it than what I've seen - Secrets, bosses, whatever else - but I played those old arcade games a lot when I was younger, and now I've grown out of them. I don't mind repetition, but I'd like to not start from the beginning every time I lose without more variation than what this game has to offer.
Devolver Digital now employs Anita Sarkesian. Don't reward developers who hire people who hate gamers. We will never forget GamerGate.
It sucks and I won't be giving devolver any more money. All their games suck. They need more diversity.
One of the main rogue likes I fall back to every now and again. This is THE GAME that put devolver digital on the map as publishers.
Don't hire Anita Sarkeesian, and if you do please say that before we buy the game. Devolver seems to hate their fanbase. I wont be buying their games or DLC's, I'm sad that i can't refund this game.

Death down barrel at you only once wink
Blur gone bullet life at the brink in a blink
Casing killing, floor clink
Fought boss, blood drink
Too wired to die too under fire to think

I can't take it anymore. I see Among Us, I think it's a gun. I see a knife, I think it's a gun. I see a man, I think he's a gun. I see the edge of reality, I think it's a gun. I look at my dog, I think he's a gun. I look at my own hands, I think they're guns. Every single thing around me, it's all guns. The walls are closing in, I can't escape the bullet hell, I can't escape the guns.
fun and  actually stimulating for someone with adhd
this feels like THE roguelike, not a single complaint, every part of this game was awesome

This Game Is Super Fun, and Isn't Hard as It Looks, the Game's Difficulty Is Perfect!
The Floors Randomizes Every Time You Die, and This Makes Every Play through Unique and Fun! There's an Infinity of Guns, Items, Passive Items, and Every of It has Great Humored References of Games, Movies, Memes that can Make you Smile, Laugh, and Like Even More The Game! Specially the Weapons/Items Description!
Every Item and Weapon Is Literally UNIQUE, I CAN'T EXPLAIN IT, HOW MANY ITEMS DOES DIFFERENT THINGS, HOW MANY FUNNY WEAPONS, BIZARRE, POP CULTURE, BANDS AND GAME REFERENCES, ALMOST ANYTHING YOU CAN IMAGINE!

This Game Teaches You How to Get Good at Hardcore Games Just Playing, and Having Fun
In the Start, You Die a Lot, Discover New Things, Mechanics, Weapons, etc
You Normally Die 9-16 Times Until You Get the Game's Pace In Floor 1, Get How Bosses Work, Learn the Dodge Feature (Essential) and When You Defeat Your First Boss... It's a Feeling! You Know You are Getting Better, You Learn to Dodge, and Get Real Life Reflexes!
When You Get In Floor 2, It Looks Like a Whole New Level, It Adds New Enemies, New Level Themed Mechanics, Like In the Mines Some Rooms have Interactive Dynamite Boxes to Explode Enemies (The Enemies Can Use it Too!) Tables to Use as Walls, or Slide on Them!!!

Best Part:
You Discover New Item/Weapon, Get Good, Die, Repeat, and Get Going, Getting Better, Until You Notice That, You Get Soo Good, The First Floor Is Nothing Hard, and You Literally Trained a Hard Game Having Fun! You Will Get Reflexes and Experience for Your Life!

Nice Features:
Some Enemies are Smart, They Can Use Interactive Objects, Others... Just try to Jump on You XD
You Can Free Npcs In Random Room Cells! They Can Sell, Give Missions, and Unlock New Items/Weapons!
There's Even Npcs That Adds New Game Modes!
Almost Every Floor Has a Gun Component, Always In the Same Room, If You Finish Your Run In Floor 5 With Them, You Have It Permanently on Lobby, If You Collect All, You Can Unlock the True Ending, and Final Levels of Each Character's Past
You Can Unlock More "Secret" Characters With Unique, and Fun Gameplay!
And Many More!

How I Got To Know Enter The Gungeon:
I Started Playing This Masterpiece In My Dear Cousin's PC (Thanks Klarfy) :3
Then, I Bough It on Nintendo Switch, and Played 60+ Hours, and Finnaly I Bough My First PC (Steam Deck) Unfortunately my Steam Deck Has Its R1 Not Working Well, So, I Can't Shoot :(
It's Almost Impossible to Fix, It Needs a New Component, and a Very Precise Component Soldering, Being Too Expensive :(
If it Wasn't by That I Would Still Play More on Deck

Final Message:
This Game Changed My Life, at Least, My Reflexed Have Got Way Better After I Played It, Now I Can Play Even More Hard Games, and Master Easily Other Games!
Thanks for Making this Game Devs :3
And Thank You For Reading My Review, My Fellow Reader :)
Be Safe Everyone!
Have a Nice Day!
A Big Hug for Y'all :3
See ya!
